tasks.title: Audit terminology and plan safe migration
tasks.objective: Map the previous product vocabulary to credit terminology across app code, tests, docs, planning artifacts, and feedback artifacts while identifying compatibility handling for older local JSON state.
authority: Classify references before editing to avoid unsafe blind replacement.
Preserve product behavior and data semantics.
Do not add feedback tasks to the main task ledger.
acceptance_criteria: The resolver has a concrete migration map for app code, tests, docs, planning artifacts, and feedback artifacts.
Durable JSON compatibility risks are identified before closeout.
Repository search confirms the previous vocabulary is no longer present as a literal.
verification: Run repository search for the previous vocabulary and record that no literal matches remain.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the migration map is clear enough to perform scoped edits safely.
tasks.evidence: 2026-06-13: Audited repo-wide terminology usage and identified durable JSON compatibility as the main migration risk. The migration map was app identifiers/copy, tests, docs/planning/agent artifacts, file/folder names, plus read compatibility for older local JSON state keys and seed grant source values.

tasks.title: Adopt credit terminology in the app
tasks.objective: Update source code, UI copy, service/test names, and app-facing behavior so the application consistently uses credit terminology.
authority: Use credit/credits in visible app terminology.
Rename internal identifiers where practical while preserving older local JSON state compatibility.
Preserve create/show-interest costs, hold/consume/refund behavior, and ledger auditability.
acceptance_criteria: User-facing app pages display credit terminology.
Tests assert credit terminology for create, interest, dashboard, refund, and purchase-adjacent copy.
Ledger behavior remains unchanged except for terminology.
Older local JSON state is normalized when read and persisted in the current credit-shaped snapshot.
verification: Run focused tests covering create, interest, dashboard, persistence ledger, and MVP verification.
tasks.area: app terminology, ledger naming, UI copy
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the app uses credit terminology and behavioral verification passes.
tasks.evidence: 2026-06-13: Renamed app constants, models, validators, repositories, ledger module, UI copy, tests, and CSS to credit terminology. Added JSON snapshot normalization for earlier local state keys and source values. Focused app tests passed.

tasks.title: Adopt credit terminology in repository artifacts
tasks.objective: Update repository documentation, planning artifacts, task ledgers, feedback artifacts, and knowledge index so non-app language consistently uses credit terminology.
authority: Update current repository language to credit terminology.
Preserve historical dates, issue IDs, task IDs, and factual evidence while changing product vocabulary.
Keep resolved feedback and knowledge-index guardrail consistency intact.
acceptance_criteria: Current docs and planning artifacts consistently describe credits.
Agent feedback and knowledge artifacts use credit terminology while preserving issue IDs, task IDs, and dates.
`npm run check:workflow` passes after resolved feedback references are updated consistently.
Repository search shows no unintended previous terminology remains.
verification: Run repository search for the previous vocabulary.
Run `npm run check` before issue closeout.
tasks.area: docs, planning, feedback workflow, knowledge index
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when repository artifacts consistently use credit terminology and workflow verification passes.
tasks.evidence: 2026-06-13: Renamed docs, planning ledgers, feedback artifacts, knowledge/progress files, and relevant issue folder names to credit terminology while preserving issue and task IDs. Workflow and full checks passed.
